title: [ipv6_addr.c:219] lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str does not null-terminate on all error paths
description:
Contract: lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str should produce valid C string or indicate error.
Violation: On snprintf encoding failure (line 253), buf[0] is set to '\0' only if buflen > 0 (lines 254-255). Same for truncation case (line 263 returns error but no explicit null-term). The initial buflen < LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN check (lines 226-230) does null-terminate.
Impact: Low - snprintf encoding failures are rare. If buflen > 0, snprintf guarantees null-termination even on truncation. The explicit buf[0] = '\0' on lines 227-229 and 254-255 is defensive but not needed since snprintf already null-terminates.
Mitigation: The code is actually correct - snprintf null-terminates. The explicit zeroing is defensive redundancy.
